title: Question of Agrawal et al. (2024)
statement_text: Can the WML$k$CIS problem be solved in polynomial time for $P_5$-free graphs (and more generally for $(P_5+rK_1)$-free graphs)?
context_text: After giving a polynomial-time algorithm for the Odd Cycle Transversal problem (the $k=2$ special case of WML$k$CIS) on $P_5$-free graphs, Agrawal et al. [1] asked whether the full WML$k$CIS problem is also tractable for $P_5$-free graphs. The present paper answers this question affirmatively and extends the result to all $(P_5+rK_1)$-free graphs for any nonnegative integer $r$.
attributed_to: Agrawal, Lima, Lokshtanov, Rzążewski, Saurabh, and Sharma
paper_contribution: Provides an explicit polynomial-time algorithm solving the Weighted Maximum List-$k$-Colourable Induced Subgraph problem for every $(P_5+rK_1)$-free graph, for any fixed $k$ and any nonnegative integer $r$.
notes: The introduction excerpt is cut off mid-sentence; the exact original phrasing of the question is not fully reproduced. Statement reconstructed from the abstract and surrounding context.
